perturbation.original_text: "Base Salary" means the Employee's total base salary as of each January 1st exclusive of special payments such as bonuses or commissions, but including any salary reductions made in accordance with Section 125 or 401(k) of the Code.
perturbation.changed_text: "Base Salary" means a discretionary amount determined annually by the Employer.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the definition of 'Base Salary' to a discretionary amount, it introduces ambiguity and contradicts the calculation method referenced in Article IV which uses 'annualized base salary at the time of death as provided by the Employer's payroll department'. This creates uncertainty on how the death benefit will be calculated.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Upon the death of the Employee while this Agreement is in force, the Employee's beneficiary as named in the Beneficiary Designation Form on page 6 (or as it may be amended according to the terms set forth on page 6) for this Agreement will be entitled to receive from the Policy proceeds an amount equal to the lesser of: (a) (two (2) times the Employee's annualized base salary at the time of death as provided by the Employer's payroll department) plus $100,000, reduced by any amount payable under the Employer's group term life insurance plan, or (b) the Net Amount At Risk.
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Base Salary" from a specific calculation to a "discretionary amount determined annually by the Employer." This contradicts the contradicted text, which relies on the "Employee's annualized base salary at the time of death as provided by the Employer's payroll department" to calculate a death benefit. The new definition makes the calculation in the contradicted text impossible or at least ambiguous, as there's no longer a defined base salary to annualize. This creates a strong in-text contradiction according to the 'Omissions - In Text Contradiction' definition provided, where essential language is removed, creating a contradiction with obligations stated in other sections.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be altered, amended or modified, including the addition of any extra policy provisions, by a written instrument signed by the Employer and the Employee.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be altered, amended or modified at the sole discretion of the Employer.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement for the Employee's signature for amendments, this modification now contradicts the premise of a mutual agreement. This is an in-text contradiction because the opening statements of the contracts speaks about the will of both parties
perturbation.contradicted_text: WHEREAS, it is now understood and agreed that this split-dollar agreement is to be effective as of the date first listed above; WHEREAS, it is now understood and agreed that this agreement shall supersede any previous split dollar agreements that are currently in place between the Employer and Employee. NOW, THEREFORE, for value received and in consideration of the mutual covenants contained herein, the parties agree as follows:
perturbation.justification: The original text requires both the Employer and Employee to sign a written instrument to alter, amend, or modify the agreement. The changed text allows the Employer to do so at their sole discretion. This directly contradicts the 'WHEREAS' clauses, which state the agreement is 'understood and agreed' by both parties, and the subsequent statement that the parties agree based on 'mutual covenants'. By removing the need for the employee's consent, the modification negates the premise of a mutual agreement and creates an in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Employer will keep possession of the Policies. The Employer agrees from time to time to make the policies available to the Employee or to the "Insurer" for the purpose of endorsing or filing any change of beneficiary on the Policies but the Policies will promptly be returned to the Employer.
perturbation.changed_text: The Employer will keep possession of the Policies. The Employer may, at its discretion, allow the Employee access to the policies.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the guaranteed access for endorsement or beneficiary changes with discretionary access, the modification introduces an in-text contradiction to the rights granted to the employee in Beneficiary Designation form. The Employee is granted exclusive rights to change the beneficiary in the last page of the contract, but the employer has the option not to give the access to the employee to make that change, as noted in ARTICLE XI.
perturbation.contradicted_location: Split Dollar Policy Endorsement Beneficiary Designation
perturbation.contradicted_text: The exclusive right to change the beneficiary for the proceeds payable under this section 2, to elect any optional method of settlement for the proceeds paid under this section 2 that are available under the terms of the policy, and to assign all rights and interests granted under this section 2 are hereby granted to the Employee. The sole signature of the Employee shall be sufficient to exercise said rights. The Owner retains all contract rights not granted to the Employee under this section 2.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces the guaranteed access to the policies for the employee to make beneficiary changes with discretionary access. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which grants the employee the 'exclusive right to change the beneficiary'. The employer's discretion to deny access now makes it impossible for the employee to exercise that exclusive right, creating a direct and impactful contradiction.
